title: Bixverse R <> Rust interface
text: bixverse also exposes Rust-accelerated set similarity calculations. illustrate performance, comparison naive R implementation Jaccard similarity across two lists. Starting sequential purrr approach baseline: Parallelising via furrr gives meaningful speedup: mirai reduces process scheduling overhead (somtimes) compared future bit better: Depending system optimised parallel R version may around 4x faster naive sequential baseline. Now compare Rust implementation: margin subtle. functions shown vignette representative sample exposed bixverse: many specialised functions throughout package worth exploring (brave can deal panic ).
code: jaccard_sim <- function(x, y) {   length(intersect(x, y)) / length(union(x, y)) }  set.seed(42L)  random_sets_1 <- purrr::map(   1:1000L,   ~ paste(sample(LETTERS, sample(1:20, 1))) )  random_sets_2 <- purrr::map(   1:500L,   ~ paste(sample(LETTERS, sample(1:20, 1))) ) tictoc::tic()  r_results <- purrr::map(   random_sets_1,   \(x) {     purrr::map_dbl(random_sets_2, \(y) jaccard_sim(x, y))   },   .progress = TRUE ) #>  ■■■                                7% |  ETA: 22s #>  ■■■■■■■                           20% |  ETA: 20s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■                       32% |  ETA: 16s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                   45% |  ETA: 13s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■                57% |  ETA: 10s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■            70% |  ETA:  7s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■        83% |  ETA:  4s #>  ■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■■    95% |  ETA:  1s  similarity_matrix <- matrix(   data = unlist(r_results),   nrow = length(random_sets_1),   ncol = length(random_sets_2),   byrow = TRUE )  tictoc::toc() #> 23.971 sec elapsed future::plan(strategy = future::multisession(workers = parallel::detectCores()))  tictoc::tic()  r_results <- furrr::future_map(   random_sets_1,   \(x) {     purrr::map_dbl(random_sets_2, \(y) jaccard_sim(x, y))   },   .progress = TRUE )  similarity_matrix <- matrix(   data = unlist(r_results),   nrow = length(random_sets_1),   ncol = length(random_sets_2),   byrow = TRUE )  tictoc::toc() #> 13.853 sec elapsed  future::plan(strategy = future::sequential()) mirai::daemons(parallel::detectCores())  tictoc::tic()  r_results_mirai <- mirai::mirai_map(   random_sets_1,   \(x, sets_2) {     purrr::map_dbl(sets_2, \(y) {       length(intersect(x, y)) / length(union(x, y))     })   },   .args = list(sets_2 = random_sets_2) )[]  similarity_matrix <- matrix(   data = unlist(r_results_mirai),   nrow = length(random_sets_1),   ncol = length(random_sets_2),   byrow = TRUE )  tictoc::toc() #> 13.161 sec elapsed  mirai::daemons(0) tictoc::tic()  rust_res <- rs_set_similarity_list2(   s_1_list = random_sets_1,   s_2_list = random_sets_2,   overlap_coefficient = FALSE )  tictoc::toc() #> 0.047 sec elapsed  all.equal(similarity_matrix, rust_res, tolerance = 1e-15) #> [1] TRUE

title: Batch correction with bixverse
text: Batch effects systematic technical differences experimental runs can obscure genuine biological signal single cell data. datasets multiple experiments, technologies, laboratories combined, cells tend group source rather cell type. Batch correction methods aim remove technical artefacts whilst preserving biological variation. bixverse provides three batch correction approaches, different trade-offs: fastMNN (Haghverdi, et al., 2018) identifies mutual nearest neighbours across batches – pairs cells different batches ’s closest match. Correction vectors computed pairs applied align embeddings. particularly effective cell type composition differs across batches. Harmony (Korsunsky, et al., 2019) operates PCA embeddings using iterative soft clustering diversity penalties. assigns cells clusters, estimates batch effects per cluster via ridge regression, corrects embedding. tends fast works well across range scenarios. BBKNN (Polanski, et al., 2020) takes fundamentally different approach. Rather correcting embedding, constructs batch-balanced k-nearest neighbour graph building separate neighbour indices per batch. corrected graph can used directly clustering visualisation. bixverse also provides three metrics assessing batch correction quality: kBET: tests whether neighbourhood batch proportions match global proportions (best embedding-based methods). Batch ASW: measures batch separation embedding space (best embedding-based methods). LISI: measures effective number batches per neighbourhood (works kNN graph).

title: Batch correction with bixverse
text: use two PBMC datasets (pbmc3k pbmc4k) standard benchmark batch correction. come tissue differ sequencing depth cell counts, introducing clear batch effect. batch correction, filter low-quality cells using mitochondrial gene proportions library complexity metrics. Let’s plot metrics:  Set cells keep Standard pre-processing: HVG selection, PCA, neighbour computation.
code: dir_data <- download_pbmc_batches()  tempdir_batch_cor <- file.path(tempdir(), "batch_cor_bixverse") dir.create(tempdir_batch_cor, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  h5ad_files <- list.files(dir_data) h5ad_files <- h5ad_files[grepl(".h5ad", h5ad_files)] h5ad_paths <- file.path(dir_data, h5ad_files) names(h5ad_paths) <- c("pbmc3k", "pbmc4k")  h5_tasks <- prescan_h5ad_files(h5_paths = h5ad_paths)  sc_object <- SingleCells(dir_data = tempdir_batch_cor)  sc_object <- load_multi_h5ad(   object = sc_object,   prescan_result = h5_tasks,   .verbose = TRUE ) #> Loading observation data from h5ad files into DuckDB. #> Loading variable data into DuckDB. var <- get_sc_var(sc_object)  gs_of_interest <- list(   MT = var[grepl("^MT-", symbol_tenx), gene_id],   Ribo = var[grepl("^RPS|^RPL", symbol_tenx), gene_id] )  sc_object <- gene_set_proportions_sc(   sc_object,   gs_of_interest,   streaming = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE )  qc_df <- sc_object[[c("cell_id", "lib_size", "nnz", "MT")]]  metrics <- list(   log10_lib_size = log10(qc_df$lib_size),   log10_nnz = log10(qc_df$nnz),   MT = qc_df$MT ) directions <- c(   log10_lib_size = "twosided",   log10_nnz = "twosided",   MT = "above" )  qc <- run_cell_qc(metrics, directions, threshold = 3)  qc #> CellQc: 7040 cells, 1199 outliers (17.0%) #> Metrics: #>   - log10_lib_size: 450 outliers [lower = 3.07, upper = 3.93] #>   - log10_nnz: 483 outliers [lower = 2.71, upper = 3.34] #>   - MT: 728 outliers [upper = 0.05] plots <- plot(qc, qc_df)  plots$log10_lib_size + plots$log10_nnz + plots$MT sc_object[["outlier"]] <- qc$combined cells_to_keep <- qc_df[!qc$combined, cell_id] sc_object <- set_cells_to_keep(sc_object, cells_to_keep) sc_object <- find_hvg_sc(   object = sc_object,   hvg_no = 2000L,   .verbose = TRUE )  sc_object <- calculate_pca_sc(   object = sc_object,   no_pcs = 30L ) #> Using dense SVD solving on scaled data on 2000 HVG.  sc_object <- find_neighbours_sc(   object = sc_object,   neighbours_params = params_sc_neighbours(     knn = list(knn_method = "exhaustive")   ) ) #>  #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph.

text: batch correction, filter low-quality cells using mitochondrial gene proportions library complexity metrics. Let’s plot metrics:  Set cells keep
code: var <- get_sc_var(sc_object)  gs_of_interest <- list(   MT = var[grepl("^MT-", symbol_tenx), gene_id],   Ribo = var[grepl("^RPS|^RPL", symbol_tenx), gene_id] )  sc_object <- gene_set_proportions_sc(   sc_object,   gs_of_interest,   streaming = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE )  qc_df <- sc_object[[c("cell_id", "lib_size", "nnz", "MT")]]  metrics <- list(   log10_lib_size = log10(qc_df$lib_size),   log10_nnz = log10(qc_df$nnz),   MT = qc_df$MT ) directions <- c(   log10_lib_size = "twosided",   log10_nnz = "twosided",   MT = "above" )  qc <- run_cell_qc(metrics, directions, threshold = 3)  qc #> CellQc: 7040 cells, 1199 outliers (17.0%) #> Metrics: #>   - log10_lib_size: 450 outliers [lower = 3.07, upper = 3.93] #>   - log10_nnz: 483 outliers [lower = 2.71, upper = 3.34] #>   - MT: 728 outliers [upper = 0.05] plots <- plot(qc, qc_df)  plots$log10_lib_size + plots$log10_nnz + plots$MT sc_object[["outlier"]] <- qc$combined cells_to_keep <- qc_df[!qc$combined, cell_id] sc_object <- set_cells_to_keep(sc_object, cells_to_keep)

title: Batch correction with bixverse

code: sc_object <- find_hvg_sc(   object = sc_object,   hvg_no = 2000L,   .verbose = TRUE )  sc_object <- calculate_pca_sc(   object = sc_object,   no_pcs = 30L ) #> Using dense SVD solving on scaled data on 2000 HVG.  sc_object <- find_neighbours_sc(   object = sc_object,   neighbours_params = params_sc_neighbours(     knn = list(knn_method = "exhaustive")   ) ) #>  #> Generating sNN graph (full: FALSE). #> Transforming sNN data to igraph.

title: Batch correction with bixverse
text: BBKNN constructs batch-balanced kNN graph rather correcting embedding. key parameter neighbours_within_batch, controls many neighbours found per batch. Setting no_neighbours_to_keep total generated neighbours (10 2 batches = 20 total) ensures distance-based filtering introduces genuine variation, making downstream metrics informative. Note kBET ASW well suited evaluating BBKNN: kBET compares neighbourhood proportions global proportions, BBKNN manipulates design, ASW requires embedding. LISI stored kNN appropriate metric .

text: HVG selection scale requires streaming = TRUE. two data passes avoid materialising large data memory. First pass calculate means standard deviations. Second pass variance stabilisation. PCA uses sparse SVD, automatically selected cell counts exceed 500k can set explicitly.
code: sc_object <- find_hvg_sc(   object = sc_object,   streaming = TRUE,   .verbose = TRUE )  sc_object <- calculate_pca_sc(   object = sc_object,   no_pcs = 30L,   sparse_svd = TRUE )

title: Scaling to millions of cells with bixverse
text: nearly 3 million cells, nearest neighbour search main computational bottleneck. companion package bixverse.gpu exposes CAGRA-style GPU-accelerated approximate nearest neighbour search makes quite fast laptop discrete GPU - thanks CubeCL WGPU long can fit data VRAM (feasible 8 GBs - think?) run quite fast. CPU-side alternatives "nndescent" "hnsw" also work considerably slower scale. (empirical reference): GPU version M1 Max 64 GB takes ~1 minute. HNSW somewhere 3 4 minutes. Likely still comparably much faster used established single cell methods terms CPU speed, yeah… GPU, go use .
code: library(bixverse.gpu)  sc_object <- find_neighbours_cagra_sc(   object = sc_object,   cagra_params = params_sc_cagra(k_query = 15L, ann_dist = "cosine"),   extract_knn = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Scaling to millions of cells with bixverse
text: Running UMAP shared nearest neighbour graph built ~2.8 million cells 65 million+ edges takes time complete single machine (embedding optimisation parallel Adam optimisation done <3 minutes ).

title: Scaling to millions of cells with bixverse
text: metadata stored obs table, subsetting cells pairwise comparison straightforward. compare two drug treatment conditions. , leverage streaming … Instead holding full data set memory, just load two groups (roughly 30k cells), fast ranking Rust DGE. Easy peezy, done ca. 3 seconds.
code: meta_data <- sc_object[[c("cell_id", "cell_name", "drug")]]  cells_grp_a <- meta_data[drug == "AT7519", cell_id] cells_grp_b <- meta_data[drug == "palbociclib", cell_id]  dge_results <- find_markers_sc(   object = sc_object,   cells_1 = cells_grp_a,   cells_2 = cells_grp_b )  setorder(dge_results, fdr)  head(dge_results, 25L)

what: Key differences from the small-data workflow
title: Scaling to millions of cells with bixverse
text: API surface intentionally identical PBMC3k walkthrough. meaningful differences working million-cell scale : Streaming /O. Use stream_h5ad instead load_h5ad, set streaming = TRUE gene_set_proportions_sc find_hvg_sc. important. also makes package actually quite cool. -disk persistence. load_existing lets reconnect previously streamed data set without re-reading .h5ad. (Btw, works data.) Sparse SVD. Automatically enabled 500k cells; can forced explicitly via sparse_svd = TRUE (slower small data sets… Modern BLAS just eats small matrix multiplications faer). GPU-accelerated kNN. bixverse.gpu provides CAGRA-based nearest neighbour search (also others) scales comfortably millions cells makes run minute . CPU versions still highly optimised SIMD memory layouts specifically designed avoid cache misses. context… Running machine took (MacBook Pro M1 Max 64 GB) took grand total ca. 20 minutes start finish, basically half time spent data /O. Memory pressure remained green whole thing.

Please ensure setting. data_name Optional string. Name data modality. snf_params List. SNF parameters, see params_snf(). list contains following elements: k - Integer. Number neighbours consider. t - Integer. Number iterations SNF algorithm. mu - Float. Normalisation factor Gaussian kernel width. alpha - Float. Normalisation parameter controlling fusion strength. normalise - Boolean. Shall continuous values Z-scored. distance_metric - String. One c("euclidean", "manhattan", "canberra", "cosine"). distance metric use continuous calculations. case pure categorical, Hamming used, mixed data types Gower distance used. parameters internally stored subsequent usage functions.

what: Calculate AUC scores (akin to AUCell) — aucell_sc
title: Calculate AUC scores (akin to AUCell) — aucell_sc
text: Calculates AUC-type score akin AUCell across gene sets, see Aibar et al. options calculate AUC. Two options : calculate proper AUROC calculations (useful marker gene expression, use "auroc" version) based Mann-Whitney statistic (useful pathway activity measurs, use "wilcox"). Data can streamed chunks 50k cells per loaded one go.

title: Calculate AUC scores (akin to AUCell) — aucell_sc
code: aucell_sc(   object,   gs_list,   auc_type = c("wilcox", "auroc"),   streaming = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate AUC scores (akin to AUCell) — aucell_sc
text: object SingleCells class. gs_list Named list. elements gene identifiers respective gene sets. auc_type String. type AUC calculate. Choice c("wilcox", "auroc"). streaming Boolean. Shall cell data streamed . Useful larger data sets. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity function.

title: Calculate AUC scores (akin to AUCell) — aucell_sc
text: AUCell results form matrix cells x gene sets.

title: Calculate AUC scores (akin to AUCell) — aucell_sc

title: Run BBKNN — bbknn_sc
text: function implements batch-balanced k-nearest neighbour algorithm Polański, et al. Briefly, algorithm generates KNN index per batch basis identifies neighbours cells individual index. Subsequently, leverages UMAP connectivity calculations reduce spurious connections. details, please refer Polański, et al.

title: Run BBKNN — bbknn_sc
code: bbknn_sc(   object,   batch_column,   no_neighbours_to_keep = 5L,   embd_to_use = "pca",   no_embd_to_use = NULL,   bbknn_params = params_sc_bbknn(),   seed = 42L,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Run BBKNN — bbknn_sc
text: object SingleCells class. batch_column String. column batch information obs data class. no_neighbours_to_keep Integer. Maximum number neighbours keep BBKNN algorithm. Due generating neighbours batch, might large number generated neighbours. keep top no_neighbours_to_keep neighbours. Defaults 5L. embd_to_use String. embedding use. Atm, option "pca". no_embd_to_use Optional integer. Number embedding dimensions use. NULL used. bbknn_params list, please see params_sc_bbknn(). list following parameters: neighbours_within_batch - Integer. Number neighbours consider per batch. set_op_mix_ratio - Numeric. Mixing ratio union (1.0) intersection (0.0). local_connectivity - Numeric. UMAP connectivity computation parameter, many nearest neighbours cell assumed fully connected. trim - Optional integer. Trim neighbours cell many top connectivities. May help population independence improve tidiness clustering. NULL, defaults 10 * neighbours_within_batch. knn - List kNN parameters. See params_knn_defaults() available parameters defaults. seed Integer. Random seed. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity function.

title: Run BBKNN — bbknn_sc
text: object added kNN matrix based BBKNN graph based returned connectivities algorithm.

title: Run BBKNN — bbknn_sc
text: Polański, et al., Bioinformatics, 2020

what: Bixverse implementation of the fgsea algorithm — calc_fgsea
title: Bixverse implementation of the fgsea algorithm — calc_fgsea
text: Rust-based version fgsea simple multi-level algorithm. Initially, simple method run. low p-values, multi-level method used estimate lower p-values possible just based permutations, see Korotkevich, et al.

title: Bixverse implementation of the fgsea algorithm — calc_fgsea
code: calc_fgsea(   stats,   pathways,   nperm = 1000L,   gsea_params = params_gsea(),   seed = 123L )

title: Bixverse implementation of the fgsea algorithm — calc_fgsea
text: stats Named numeric vector. gene level statistic. pathways List. named list element containing genes pathway. nperm Integer. Number permutation tests. Defaults 2000L. gsea_params List. GSEA parameters, see params_gsea() wrapper function. function generates list containing: min_size - Integer. Minimum size gene sets. max_size - Integer. Maximum size gene sets. gsea_param - Float. GSEA parameter. Defaults 1.0. sample_size - Integer. Number samples iterate multi-level implementation fgsea. eps - Float. Boundary calculating p-value. Used multi- level implementation fgsea. seed Random seed reproducibility.

title: Bixverse implementation of the fgsea algorithm — calc_fgsea
text: data.table results GSEA following columns: es - Float. enrichment score pathway. nes - Float. normalised enrichment score pathway. pvals - Float. p-value pathway. n_more_extreme - Integer. Number permutation extreme enrichment scores actual. size - Integer. size pathway. pathway_name - Character. name pathway. leading_edge - List character vectors leading edge genes. fdr - Float. adjusted pval.

title: Bixverse implementation of the fgsea algorithm — calc_fgsea
text: Korotkevich, et al., bioRxiv

title: Bixverse implementation of GSVA — calc_gsva
text: exp Numerical matrix. Rows represents features, columns features/genes. pathways List. named list element containing genes pathway. gaussian Boolean. set TRUE Gaussian kernel used, FALSE Poisson used. gsva_params List. GSVA parameters, see params_gsva() wrapper function. function generates list containing: tau - Float. tau parameter algorithm. Large values emphasise tails . Defaults 1.0. min_size - Integer. Minimum size gene sets. max_size - Integer. Maximum size gene sets. max_diff - Boolean. Influences scoring. TRUE difference used; FALSE, largest absolute value. abs_rank - Boolean. TRUE = pos-neg, FALSE = pos+neg internal calculations. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity.

what: Calculate batch average silhouette width — calculate_batch_asw_sc
title: Calculate batch average silhouette width — calculate_batch_asw_sc
text: Computes average silhouette width (ASW) batch labels using pairwise Euclidean distances embedding space. cell, function estimates mean distance cells batch () mean distance cells nearest batch (b), computes s = (b - ) / max(, b). Values near 0 indicate good batch mixing, values near 1 indicate batch separation, negative values suggest overcorrection. metric best suited embedding-based correction methods (e.g. Harmony, fastMNN). graph-based methods like BBKNN, consider using calculate_batch_lisi_sc() instead.

title: Calculate batch average silhouette width — calculate_batch_asw_sc
code: calculate_batch_asw_sc(   object,   batch_column,   embd_to_use = "pca",   max_cells = 5000L,   seed = 42L,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate batch average silhouette width — calculate_batch_asw_sc
text: object SingleCells class. batch_column String. column batch information obs data class. embd_to_use String. embedding compute ASW . One c("pca", "harmony", "mnn"). Defaults "pca". max_cells Integer NULL. NULL, subsample many cells performance. pairwise distance computation O(n^2), subsampling recommended large datasets. Defaults 5000L. seed Integer. Seed subsampling reproducibility. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity function.

title: Calculate batch average silhouette width — calculate_batch_asw_sc
text: BatchSilhouetteScores object following elements per_cell - Per-cell silhouette scores [-1, 1]. mean_asw - Mean silhouette width across cells. median_asw - Median silhouette width across cells. n_batches - Number batches data. embedding_used - embedding ASW computed .

title: Calculate batch LISI scores — calculate_batch_lisi_sc
text: Computes Local Inverse Simpson's Index (LISI) batch labels using kNN graph. LISI measures effective number batches represented cell's neighbourhood. perfect mixing, LISI equals number batches. mixing, LISI equals 1. Unlike kBET, LISI compare global batch proportions, making suitable graph-based correction methods like BBKNN.

title: Calculate batch LISI scores — calculate_batch_lisi_sc
Calculate batch LISI scores — calculate_batch_lisi_sc
text: object SingleCells class. batch_column String. column batch information obs data class. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity function.

title: Calculate batch LISI scores — calculate_batch_lisi_sc
text: BatchLisiScores object following elements per_cell - Per-cell LISI scores [1, n_batches]. mean_lisi - Mean LISI across cells. median_lisi - Median LISI across cells. n_batches - Number batches data.

title: Calculate batch LISI scores — calculate_batch_lisi_sc

title: Calculate kBET scores — calculate_kbet_sc
text: function calculates k-nearest neighbour batch-effect test (kBET). Briefly, function leverages Chi-Square statistic calculate differences batch proportions observed neighbourhood given cell overall batch proportions. test significant cell indicates poor mixing cell specifically. Large number positive tests indicate bad mixing overall. details, please see Büttner et al.

Calculate kBET scores — calculate_kbet_sc
text: object SingleCells class. batch_column String. column batch information obs data class. threshold Numeric. Number 0 1. threshold, test considered significant. Defaults 0.05. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity function.

title: Calculate kBET scores — calculate_kbet_sc
text: KbetScores object following elements kbet_score - Proportion significant tests cells. 0 indicates perfect mixing, 1 indicates mixing batches. significant_tests - Logical vector indicating cells test threshold. p_values - p-values Chi-Square test. chi_square_stats - Per-cell Chi-Square statistics. mean_chi_square - Mean Chi-Square statistic across cells. median_chi_square - Median Chi-Square statistic across cells. threshold - significance threshold used. n_batches - Number batches data.

text: Büttner, et al., Nat. Methods, 2019

text: Calculate TOM correlation matrix

title: Calculate the TOM from a correlation matrix — calculate_tom
Calculate the TOM from a correlation matrix — calculate_tom
text: cor_mat Numerical matrix. symmetric correlation matrix. signed Boolean. want calculate signed version. set FALSE, absolute correlation coefficients used. version String. One c("v1", "v2"). Defaults "v1".

title: Calculate the TOM from a correlation matrix — calculate_tom
text: symmetric matrix dimensions cor_mat containing topological overlap measures.

title: Calculate the TOM from a correlation matrix — calculate_tom
text:
Calculates topological overlap matrix correlation matrix. TOM defined : Unsigned, v1: $$TOM_{ij} =  rac{a_{ij} + \sum_{k 
eq ,j} a_{ik} a_{kj}}{\min(k_i, k_j) + 1 - a_{ij}}$$ Signed, v1: $$TOM_{ij} =  rac{a_{ij} + \sum_{k 
eq ,j} a_{ik} a_{kj}}{\min(k_i, k_j) + 1 - \left|a_{ij}ight|}$$ Unsigned, v2: $$TOM_{ij} = 0.5 \left( a_{ij} +  rac{\sum_{k 
eq ,j} a_{ik} a_{kj}}{\min(k_i, k_j) + a_{ij}} ight)$$ Signed, v2: $$TOM_{ij} = 0.5 \left( a_{ij} +  rac{\sum_{k 
eq ,j} a_{ik} a_{kj}}{\min(k_i, k_j) + \left|a_{ij}ight|} ight)$$ \(a_{ij}\) affinity nodes \(\) \(j\), \(k_i = \sum_j a_{ij}\) connectivity node \(\). signed networks, connectivity calculated \(k_i = \sum_j \left|a_{ij}ight|\). Version 2 uses different normalization approach scales shared neighbor contribution separately combining direct connection strength.

eq ,j} a_{ik} a_{kj}}{\min(k_i, k_j) + 1 - \left|a_{ij}ight|}$$ Unsigned, v2: $$TOM_{ij} = 0.5 \left( a_{ij} +  rac{\sum_{k 
eq ,j} a_{ik} a_{kj}}{\min(k_i, k_j) + a_{ij}} ight)$$ Signed, v2: $$TOM_{ij} = 0.5 \left( a_{ij} +  rac{\sum_{k 
eq ,j} a_{ik} a_{kj}}{\min(k_i, k_j) + \left|a_{ij}ight|} ight)$$ \(a_{ij}\) affinity nodes \(\) \(j\), \(k_i = \sum_j a_{ij}\) connectivity node \(\). signed networks, connectivity calculated \(k_i = \sum_j \left|a_{ij}ight|\). title: Identify privileged communities based on a given diffusion vector — community_detection
text: object NetworkDiffusions object. underlying class NetworkDiffusions(). community_params List. Parameters community detection within reduced network, see params_community_detection(). list following items: max_nodes - Integer. Number maximum nodes per community. Larger communities recursively subclustered. min_nodes - Integer. Minimum number nodes per community. min_seed_nodes - Integer. Minimum number seed genes found given community. initial_res - Float. Initial resolution parameter Leiden clustering. threshold_type - String. One c("prop_based", "pval_based"). can chose include certain proportion network (like original paper) highest diffusion scores, use p-values based permutations. Defaults "prop_based". network_threshold - Float. proportion network include. Used threshold_type = "prop_based". pval_threshold - Float. maximum p-value nodes included. Used threshold_type = "pval_based". seed Random seed. .verbose Controls verbosity function. .max_iters Controls many iterations shall tried sub-clustering. note, iteration sub-clustering, resolution parameter increased 0.05, identify granular communities within sub communities.

what: Find the neighbours for single cell. — find_neighbours_sc
title: Find the neighbours for single cell. — find_neighbours_sc
text: function generate kNNs based given embedding. Four different algorithms implemented different speed accuracy approximate nearest neighbours. "hnsw" implements Hierarchical Navigatable Small Worlds vector search slower index generation high precision. "annoy" based Approximate Nearest Neighbours Oh Yeah algorithm rapid terms index generation, querying large data sets can slow. "nndescent" Rust-based implementation PyNNDescent algorithm good -rounder performs well large data sets. "exhaustive" performs exact nearest neighbour search. Subsequently, kNN data used generate sNN igraph clustering methods.

Shall full shared nearest neighbour graph generated generates edges cells instead neighbours. pruning - Numeric. Weights threshold set 0 generation sNN graph. snn_similarity - String. One c("rank", "jaccard"). Defines weight SNN graph calculated. details, please see params_sc_neighbours(). knn - List kNN parameters. See params_knn_defaults() available parameters defaults. seed Integer. reproducibility. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity returns run times.

what: Generate the KNN data with distances — generate_knn_sc

text: function generate kNNs based given embedding. Three different algorithms implemented different speed accuracy approximate nearest neighbours. "annoy" rapid based Approximate Nearest Neigbours Oh Yeah algorithm; "hnsw" implements Hierarchical Navigatable Small Worlds vector search slower, precise. Lastly, option "nndescent", Rust-based implementation PyNNDescent algorithm. version skips index generation can faster smaller data sets. version function returns sc_knn object can used functions.

what: Generate an miloR abundance object for differential abundance testing — get_miloR_abundances_sc
title: Generate an miloR abundance object for differential abundance testing — get_miloR_abundances_sc
text: function implements miloR differential abundance testing top kNN graph. general idea approach use kNN graph generated single cell data, generate representative neighbourhoods calculate differential abundances within neighbourhoods. details method, please refer Dann, et al. function take SingleCells class, run neighbourhood detection, count occurrences sample return sc_miloR class subsequent differential abundance testing annotations.

title: Generate an miloR abundance object for differential abundance testing — get_miloR_abundances_sc
code: get_miloR_abundances_sc(   object,   sample_id_col,   embd_to_use = "pca",   no_embd_to_use = NULL,   miloR_params = params_sc_miloR(),   seed = 42L,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Generate an miloR abundance object for differential abundance testing — get_miloR_abundances_sc
text: object SingleCells class. sample_id_col Character. column obs table representing sample identifier count. embd_to_use Character. embedding use refinement procedure. Please use used generate neighbours! Defaults "pca". no_embd_to_use Optional integer. want use subset embedding. miloR_params list, please see params_sc_miloR(). list following parameters: prop - Numeric. Proportion cells sample neighbourhood indices. Must (0,1). k_refine - Integer. Number neighbours use refinement. refinement_strategy - String. Strategy refining sampled indices. One c("approximate", "bruteforce", "index"). index_type - String. Type kNN index use. One c("annoy", "hnsw"). knn - List kNN parameters. See params_knn_defaults() available parameters defaults. Note: knn_method "exhaustive" MiloR basically boils "bruteforce". seed Integer. Seed reproducibility .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity method.

title: Generate an miloR abundance object for differential abundance testing — get_miloR_abundances_sc
text: Dann, et al., Nat Biotechnol, 2022

what: Run gene ontology enrichment with elimination method. — gse_go_elim_method
title: Run gene ontology enrichment with elimination method. — gse_go_elim_method
text: method takes GeneOntologyElim target gene set performs GSE enrichment leveraging ontological information. starts lowest levels ontology tests significant enrichment GO terms. threshold p-value elimination threshold, genes term removed ancestors. function proceeds next level ontology repeats process. gene universe automatically set every gene represented ontology.

what: Identify the TF to gene regulation — identify_tf_to_genes
title: Identify the TF to gene regulation — identify_tf_to_genes
text: Generates TF gene associations importance matrix produced tree-based regression models. Two filtering strategies available: Threshold (method = "threshold"): gene, computes mean + n_sd * SD importance scores across TFs retains pairs exceeding threshold. adapts per-gene importance distribution less sensitive differences learners. Top-k (method = "top_k"): Selects top k_tfs TFs per gene /top k_genes genes per TF. margins can combined (union). least one k_tfs k_genes must provided. methods accept optional min_importance floor.

title: Identify the TF to gene regulation — identify_tf_to_genes
code: identify_tf_to_genes(   x,   method = c("threshold", "top_k"),   k_tfs = NULL,   k_genes = NULL,   n_sd = 2,   min_importance = NULL,   .verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'ScenicGrn' identify_tf_to_genes(   x,   method = c("threshold", "top_k"),   k_tfs = NULL,   k_genes = NULL,   n_sd = 2,   min_importance = NULL,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Identify the TF to gene regulation — identify_tf_to_genes
text: x ScenicGrn object. method Character. Either "top_k" "threshold". k_tfs Optional integer. Top TFs per gene (used method = "top_k"). k_genes Optional integer. Top genes per TF (used method = "top_k"). n_sd Numeric. Number standard deviations per-gene mean use threshold (used method = "threshold"). Default 2. min_importance Optional numeric [0, 1]. Absolute minimum importance score inclusion. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity function.

what: Wrapper function for Boost parameters — params_boost
title: Wrapper function for Boost parameters — params_boost
text: Wrapper function Boost parameters

title: Wrapper function for Boost parameters — params_boost
code: params_boost(   boost_rate = 0.25,   replace = FALSE,   resolution = 1,   n_iters = 25L,   p_thresh = 1e-07,   voter_thresh = 0.9,   normalisation = list(),   hvg = list(),   pca = list(),   knn = list(k = 0L, ann_dist = "euclidean") )

title: Wrapper function for Boost parameters — params_boost
text: boost_rate Numeric. Boosting rate algorithm. Must 0 1. Defaults 0.25. replace Boolean. Whether use replacement boosting. Defaults FALSE. resolution Numeric. Resolution parameter graph-based clustering. Higher values lead clusters. Defaults 1.0. n_iters Integer. Number iterations run algorithm. Defaults 25L. p_thresh Numeric. P-value threshold significance testing. Defaults 1e-7. voter_thresh Numeric. Voter threshold across iterations. Proportion iterations cell must assigned cluster considered member. Must 0 1. Defaults 0.9. normalisation List. Optional overrides normalisation parameters. See params_norm_doublet_detection_defaults() available parameters: log_transform, mean_center, normalise_variance, target_size. Note: Boost uses different defaults (log_transform = FALSE, mean_center = TRUE, normalise_variance = TRUE, target_size = NULL). hvg List. Optional overrides highly variable gene selection parameters. See params_hvg_defaults() available parameters: min_gene_var_pctl, hvg_method, loess_span, clip_max. pca List. Optional overrides PCA parameters. See params_pca_defaults() available parameters: no_pcs, random_svd. knn List. Optional overrides kNN parameters. See params_knn_defaults() available parameters: k, knn_method, ann_dist, search_budget, n_trees, delta, diversify_prob, ef_budget, m, ef_construction, ef_search, n_list n_probe. Note: function defaults k = 0L (automatic neighbour detection).

title: Wrapper function for Boost parameters — params_boost
text: named list Boost parameters, combining defaults user-specified overrides. list Boost parameters.

what: Helper function to generate kNN defaults — params_knn_defaults
title: Helper function to generate kNN defaults — params_knn_defaults
text: function generates various sensible default parameters different approximate nearest neighbours available within package.

title: Helper function to generate kNN defaults — params_knn_defaults
Helper function to generate kNN defaults — params_knn_defaults
text: list default parameters kNN searches. Following parameters: k - Number neighbours. Defaults 15L. knn_method - method use approximate nearest neighbour search. Defaults "hnsw". implementations : c("hnsw", "annoy", "nndescent", "ivf", "exhaustive"). ann_dist - distance metric use approximate nearest neighbour search. Defaults "cosine". implementations c("cosine", "euclidean"). n_trees - Annoy param: number trees generate Annoy. Defaults 50L. search_budget - Annoy param: optional search budget per tree Annoy. provided, default n_tree * k * 20L. diversify_prob - NNDescent param: diversification probability NNDescent index. diversify index end identify potentiall better edges. Defaults 0.0. delta - NNDescent param: early termination criterium NNDescent. Defaults 0.001. ef_budget - NNDescent param: optional query budget parameter. Can accelerate querying, cost Recall. m - HNSW param: number connections layers HNSW. Defaults 16L. ef_construction - HNSW param: size dynamic candidate list construction. Defaults 200L. ef_search - HNSW param: size candidate list (higher = better recall, slower). Defaults 100L. n_list - IVF param: number clusters/centroids generate. Defaults NULL (sqrt(n) n_lists generated case). n_probe - IVF param: number clusters/centroids query Defaults NULL (sqrt(n_lists) clusters queried case).

Defaults "average". max_hops Integer NULL. provided, restricts label spreading nodes within many hops labelled node. Nodes beyond limit remain -zeroes. Defaults NULL.

Jaccard similarity calculates Jaccard neighbours, whereas rank method calculates edge weights based ranking shared neighbours. rank method, weight determined finding shared neighbour lowest combined rank across cells, lower-ranked (closer) shared neighbours result higher edge weights methods produce weights normalised range [0, 1]. knn List. title: Constructor for SCENIC parameters — params_scenic
text: min_counts Integer. Minimum total counts gene needs included analysis. Defaults 50L. min_cells Numeric. Minimum proportion cells (0 1) must express gene considered. Defaults 0.03. learner_type Character. Regression learner use. One "randomforest", "extratrees", "grnboost2". Defaults "randomforest". gene_batch_strategy Character. Strategy grouping target genes batches. One "random" "correlated". used "randomforest" "extratrees" learners; ignored "grnboost2". Defaults "correlated". gene_batch_size Optional integer. Number genes per batch. NULL (default), batch size determined automatically. Ignored "grnboost2". n_pcs Integer. Number PCs use correlated gene batch strategy. Defaults 50L. n_subsample Integer. Cell subsampling threshold correlated gene batch strategy. number cells meets exceeds value, n_subsample cells randomly selected prior running randomised SVD. Defaults 100000L. learner_params List. Optional overrides regression learner parameters. "randomforest", see params_scenic_random_forest_defaults(). "extratrees", see params_scenic_extra_trees_defaults(). "grnboost2", see params_scenic_gradient_boosting_defaults().

title: Wrapper function for Scrublet doublet detection parameters — params_scrublet
text: sim_doublet_ratio Numeric. Number doublets simulate relative number observed cells. example, 2.0 simulates twice many doublets cells. Defaults 1.5. expected_doublet_rate Numeric. Expected doublet rate experiment, typically 0.05-0.10 depending cell loading. Must 0 1. Defaults 0.1. stdev_doublet_rate Numeric. Uncertainty expected doublet rate. Defaults 0.02. n_bins Integer. Number bins histogram-based automatic threshold detection. Typically 50-100. Defaults 100L. manual_threshold Optional numeric. Manual doublet score threshold. NULL (default), threshold automatically detected simulated doublet score distribution. normalisation List. Optional overrides normalisation parameters. See params_norm_doublet_detection_defaults() available parameters: log_transform, mean_center, normalise_variance, target_size. hvg List. Optional overrides highly variable gene selection parameters. See params_hvg_defaults() available parameters: min_gene_var_pctl, hvg_method, loess_span, clip_max. pca List. Optional overrides PCA parameters. See params_pca_defaults() available parameters: no_pcs, random_svd, sparse skip_first_pc. knn List. Optional overrides kNN parameters. See params_knn_defaults() available parameters: k, knn_method, ann_dist, search_budget, n_trees, delta, diversify_prob, ef_budget, m, ef_construction, ef_search, n_list n_probe. Note: function defaults k = 0L (automatic neighbour detection).

title: Generates the kNN graph — rs_sc_knn

Good compromise index generation, querying speed. "hnsw": uses hierarchical navigatable small worlds index hood. index generation takes long, higher recall ideal large datasets due subdued memory pressure. "nndescent": index-free approximate nearest neighbour algorithm ideal small, ephemeral kNN graphs.

title: Sparsify bulkRNAseq like data — rs_simulate_dropouts
text: function takes (raw) count matrix (example synthetic data bixverse) applies sparsification based two possible functions: Logistic function: dropout probability defined :   following characteristics: Plateaus global_sparsity dropout high expression genes Partial dropout preserves count structure via binomial thinning Good preserving variance-mean relationships Power Decay function: dropout probability defined :   following characteristics: plateau - high expression genes get substantial dropout Complete dropout (partial dropout) uniform dropout across expression range
code: P(dropout) = clamp(1 / (1 + exp(shape * (ln(exp+1) - ln(midpoint+1)))), 0.3, 0.8) * (1 - global_sparsity) + global_sparsity P(dropout) = (midpoint / (exp + midpoint))^power * scale_factor * (1 - global_sparsity) + global_sparsity

title: Sparsify bulkRNAseq like data — rs_simulate_dropouts
code: rs_simulate_dropouts(   count_mat,   dropout_function,   dropout_midpoint,   dropout_shape,   power_factor,   global_sparsity,   seed )

title: Sparsify bulkRNAseq like data — rs_simulate_dropouts
text: count_mat Numerical matrix. Original numeric matrix. dropout_function String. One c("log", "powerdecay"). Defines function used induce sparsity. dropout_midpoint Numeric. Controls midpoint parameter logistic power decay function. dropout_shape Numeric. Controls shape parameter logistic function. power_factor Numeric. Controls power factor power decay function. global_sparsity Numeric. global sparsity parameter. seed Integer. Seed reproducibility.

title: Sparsify bulkRNAseq like data — rs_simulate_dropouts
text: sparsified matrix based provided parameters.

what: Main function to run CisTarget — run_cistarget
title: Main function to run CisTarget — run_cistarget
text: bixverse implementation RCisTarget workflow, one algorithms used SCENIC, see Aibar, et al. need motif target gene rankings, see read_motif_ranking() motif TF annotations, see read_motif_annotation_file().

title: Main function to run CisTarget — run_cistarget
code: run_cistarget(   gs_list,   rankings,   annot_data,   cis_target_params = params_cistarget() )

title: Main function to run CisTarget — run_cistarget
text: gs_list Named list character vectors. element gene set containing gene identifiers must match row names rankings. rankings Integer matrix. Motif rankings genes. Row names gene identifiers, column names motif identifiers. Lower values indicate higher regulatory potential. annot_data data.table. Motif annotation database mapping motifs transcription factors. Must contain columns: motif, TF, annotationSource. cis_target_params List. Output params_cistarget(): auc_threshold - Numeric. Proportion genes use AUC threshold calculation. Default 0.05 means top 5 percent genes. nes_threshold - Numeric. Normalised Enrichment Score threshold determining significant motifs. Default 3.0. rcc_method - Character. Recovery curve calculation method: "approx" (approximate, faster) "icistarget" (exact, slower). high_conf_cats - Character vector. Annotation categories considered high confidence (e.g., "directAnnotation", "inferredBy_Orthology"). low_conf_cats - Character vector. Annotation categories considered lower confidence (e.g., "inferredBy_MotifSimilarity").

title: Main function to run CisTarget — run_cistarget
text: data.table enriched motifs corresponding statistics high & low confidence TFs gene set.

TF indices intersected target gene indices TFs passing gene filter excluded predictor set remain potential targets present genes_to_take. option generate TF-gene importance values three distinct methods. random_forest extratrees version, batching strategy applied default settings. Correlated genes identified clustered together via k-means clustering feature loadings PCA. divided batches gene_batch_size regression learners leveraging multi-target regression fit genes batch one go. massively accelerates algorithm importance values per gene-TF pair calculated individually. Due batching similar gene, signal dilution limited. wish run traditional approach, can set gene_batch_size 1L use grnboost2 learner can fit one gene given time.

title: Doublet detection with Scrublet — scrublet_sc
text: function implements doublet detection Scrublet, see Wolock, et al. Briefly, arteficial doublets generated data via random combination initial cells. Highly variable genes (HVG) identified observed cells projected PCA space. Subsequently, simulated doublets projected PCA space given HVGs; kNN graphs generated kNN classifier used assign probability given cell original data doublet. details, please check publication.

title: Doublet detection with Scrublet — scrublet_sc
code: scrublet_sc(   object,   scrublet_params = params_scrublet(),   seed = 42L,   streaming = FALSE,   return_combined_pca = FALSE,   return_pairs = FALSE,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Doublet detection with Scrublet — scrublet_sc
text: object SingleCells class. scrublet_params list final scrublet parameters, see params_scrublet() full details. seed Integer. Random seed. streaming Boolean. Shall streaming used HVG calculations. Slower, less memory usage. return_combined_pca Boolean. Shall PCA observed cells simulated doublets returned. return_pairs Boolean. Shall pairs returned. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity function.

title: Doublet detection with Scrublet — scrublet_sc
text: scrublet_res class following items: predicted_doublets - Boolean vector indicating observed cells predicted doublets (TRUE = doublet, FALSE = singlet). doublet_scores_obs - Numerical vector likelihood doublet observed cells. doublet_scores_sim - Numerical vector likelihood doublet simulated cells. doublet_errors_obs - Numerical vector standard errors scores observed cells. z_scores - Z-scores observed cells. Represents: score - threshold / error. threshold - Used threshold. detected_doublet_rate - Fraction cells called doublet. detectable_doublet_fraction - Fraction simulated doublets scores threshold. overall_doublet_rate - Estimated overall doublet rate. roughly match expected doublet rate. pca - Optional PCA embeddings across original cells simulated doublets. pair_1 - Optional index parent cell 1 simulated doublets. pair_2 - Optional index parent cell 2 simulated doublets.

title: Doublet detection with Scrublet — scrublet_sc
text: Wollock, et al., Cell Syst, 2020

what: Simulate dropouts via different functions on synthetic bulk data — simulate_dropouts
title: Simulate dropouts via different functions on synthetic bulk data — simulate_dropouts
text: function induces expression-level dependent sparsity data. two possible functions : Logistic function: dropout probability defined :   following characteristics: Plateaus global_sparsity dropout high expression genes Partial dropout preserves count structure via binomial thinning Good preserving variance-mean relationships Power Decay function: dropout probability defined :   following characteristics: plateau - high expression genes get substantial dropout Complete dropout (partial dropout) uniform dropout across expression range
code: P(dropout) = clamp(1 / (1 + exp(shape * (ln(exp+1) - ln(midpoint+1)))), 0.3, 0.8) * (1 - global_sparsity) + global_sparsity P(dropout) = (midpoint / (exp + midpoint))^power * scale_factor * (1 - global_sparsity) + global_sparsity

title: Simulate dropouts via different functions on synthetic bulk data — simulate_dropouts

what: Test neighbourhoods for differential abundance — test_nhoods
title: Test neighbourhoods for differential abundance — test_nhoods
text: Performs differential abundance testing single-cell neighbourhoods using edgeR's quasi-likelihood negative binomial framework. function fits generalised linear model neighbourhood cell counts, tests differential abundance conditions, applies spatial FDR correction account overlapping neighbourhoods. implementation follows approach described Dann et al., using graph-based neighbourhoods identify regions significant compositional changes single-cell data.

title: Test neighbourhoods for differential abundance — test_nhoods
code: test_nhoods(   x,   design,   design_df,   coef = NULL,   norm_method = c("TMM", "RLE", "logMS"),   min_mean = 0,   robust = TRUE,   fdr_weighting = c("k-distance", "graph-overlap", "none") )  # S3 method for class 'miloR' test_nhoods(   x,   design,   design_df,   coef = NULL,   norm_method = c("TMM", "RLE", "logMS"),   min_mean = 0,   robust = TRUE,   fdr_weighting = c("k-distance", "graph-overlap", "none") )

title: Test neighbourhoods for differential abundance — test_nhoods
text: x miloR object run differential abundance analysis. design Formula experimental design design_df data.frame. Contains metadata used generation model matrix. coef Optional string/integer. complex experimental designs, can specify coefficient test. NULL, tests last coefficient design matrix (typically main effect interest). norm_method String. Normalisation method use. One c("TMM", "RLE", "logMS"). Defaults TMM (trimmed mean M-values). min_mean Numeric. Minimum mean count threshold filtering neighbourhoods. Neighbourhoods mean counts value excluded. Defaults 0 (filtering). robust Logical. TRUE, uses robust estimation quasi-likelihood dispersion. Recommended datasets potential outliers. Defaults TRUE. fdr_weighting String. Spatial FDR weighting scheme. One c("k-distance", "graph-overlap", "none"). k-distance uses distance k-th nearest neighbour, graph-overlap uses neighbourhood overlap counts. Defaults k-distance.

title: Test neighbourhoods for differential abundance — test_nhoods
text: miloR object added model results differential abundance analysis.

title: Run the SCENIC motif enrichment — tf_to_genes_motif_enrichment
code: tf_to_genes_motif_enrichment(   x,   motif_rankings,   annot_data,   cis_target_params = params_cistarget(),   gene_id_to_symbol = NULL,   only_high_conf_tf = TRUE,   .verbose = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'ScenicGrn' tf_to_genes_motif_enrichment(   x,   motif_rankings,   annot_data,   cis_target_params = params_cistarget(),   gene_id_to_symbol = NULL,   only_high_conf_tf = TRUE,   .verbose = TRUE )

title: Run the SCENIC motif enrichment — tf_to_genes_motif_enrichment
text: x ScenicGrn object generate TF gene associations. motif_rankings Integer matrix. Motif rankings genes. Row names gene identifiers, column names motif identifiers. Lower values indicate higher regulatory potential. annot_data data.table. Motif annotation database mapping motifs transcription factors. Must contain columns: motif, TF, annotationSource. cis_target_params List. Output params_cistarget(): auc_threshold - Numeric. Proportion genes use AUC threshold calculation. Default 0.05 means top 5 percent genes. nes_threshold - Numeric. Normalised Enrichment Score threshold determining significant motifs. Default 3.0. rcc_method - Character. Recovery curve calculation method: "approx" (approximate, faster) "icistarget" (exact, slower). high_conf_cats - Character vector. Annotation categories considered high confidence (e.g., "directAnnotation", "inferredBy_Orthology"). low_conf_cats - Character vector. Annotation categories considered lower confidence (e.g., "inferredBy_MotifSimilarity"). gene_id_to_symbol Named character vector. Mapping gene identifiers used internally (e.g., Ensembl IDs) identifiers used motif rankings (e.g., HGNC symbols). Names internal IDs, values ranking IDs. NULL (default), mapping applied internal IDs assumed match ranking rownames. only_high_conf_tf Boolean. Shall high confidence TF motif association used. Defaults TRUE. .verbose Boolean. Controls verbosity function.

title: Run the SCENIC motif enrichment — tf_to_genes_motif_enrichment
text: Adds data.table first tf gene results class.

title: bixverse 0.3.0
text: big one… First, shift versioning + release full single cell analysis suite. See details. Full refactor Rust code, lives crate Class getters leverages -disk storage counts streaming engine enable memory-friendly analysis millions cells. /O helpers can read mtx outputs, h5ad, h5ad normalised counts, multiple h5ad conversion Seurat implemented. Fully functional suite pre-processing functions: proportion gene sets, proportion Top N genes overall counts, HVG detection, PCA (sparse solving avoiding densification), multiple kNN backends different use-cases, sNN generation Leiden community detection. Three different batch correction methods implemented: BBKNN, fastMNN Harmony. Various analysis methods single cell implemented: AUCell, GeneModuleScoring, VISION, Hotspot, SCENIC miloR implementations. Metacell generation: hdWGCNA bootstrap approach, SuperCells SEACells. Large number convenience functions. Sister package started plotting functions. Package fast 2D embedding methods also ready sister package – check manifoldsR.

title: bixverse 0.0.2.3
text: RCisTarget implemented Rust, see Aebir et al. details. Bug fix: scaling preprocess_bulk_coexp() caused matrix transpose.

title: bixverse 0.0.2.2
text: Mitch multi contrast enrichment implemented Rust. Rework refactor sub modules Rust code. Additional Rust functions set similarity across list items R. QC function bulk DGE class split qc_bulk_dge() normalise_bulk_dge(). Bulk DGE function split calculate_dge_limma() calculate_dge_hedges(). Splitting correlation sign Eigengene calculation added CoReMo. SNF algorithm added Wang et al.. Spectral clustering methods implemented Rust. Breaking change: rs_set_similarity_list() renamed rs_set_similarity_list2(). former now takes single list calculates set similarity across permutations elements. Breaking change: preprocess_bulk_dge() calculate_all_dges() removed.

title: bixverse 0.0.2.1
text: Constrained PageRank implementation added. Improved synthetic data correlation-based gene module detection. Improved tests ICA, CoReMo graph-based correlation modules. Various distance calculations accelerated Rust. Rust-based personalised PageRank implementations now support weighted graphs. Bug fixes graph-based correlation methods. Fixed export bugs plotting functions. Breaking change: interface Rust-based personalised PageRank calculations may break due addition weights parameter.

title: bixverse 0.0.2.0
text: Rework ontology class Wang similarity added additional measure. Rework genetic community detection class diffusion methods, including permutation-based testing diffusions. Rework ICA code testing suite added ICA class. Reciprocal best hit method based correlations added; additional parameter now required specifying whether use set similarity correlation-based similarity. Vignettes added various methods. GSVA ssGSEA implemented Rust. Rust code reworked various places improved performance. Mutual information calculations implemented Rust. Improvements synthetic data. Bug fixes graph-based correlation methods. Breaking change: ontology class functions methods modified heavily may breaking. Breaking change: community detection method updated; old code may work. Breaking change: ICA detection functions methods updated may break existing code.

title: bixverse 0.0.1.4
text: TOM implemented Rust correlation-based module detection. CoReMo-based gene module detection added. Breaking change: cor_module_check_res() renamed cor_module_graph_check_res() cor_module_final_modules() renamed cor_module_graph_final_modules().

title: bixverse 0.0.1.3
text: fgsea multi level method implemented; n_more_extreme now returned GSEA functions. RBF function implementations added R matrices.

title: bixverse 0.0.1.2
text: fgsea simple implemented gene ontology elimination method. Improved gene ontology elimination methods reduce unnecessary copying.

title: bixverse 0.0.1.1
text: Semantic similarities ontologies added. Speed improvements various Rust functions via reduced memory copying use lifetimes. Package stability improved tests powered tinytest. Set similarity Rust functions exposed. DGE class added Limma Voom differential gene expression calculations. Wrapper functions added load h5ad objects R memory. Traditional GSEA fgsea simple ported Rust. Bug fix: future::plan() iterating resolutions reciprocal best hit graph generation. Bug fix: hypergeometric calculations RBH graph. Breaking change: community_detection() now uses params_community_detection() parameters.

title: bixverse 0.0.1.0
text: Hypergeometric tests gene set analysis single lists target gene sets. Gene ontology aware hypergeometric test GO analysis. Network diffusion methods based personalised PageRank (single tied diffusion). Community detection algorithms top diffusion scores. Reciprocal best hit graphs using set similarities gene modules different data sets methods. Contrastive PCA implementation gene module detection. Differential correlation-based methods gene module detection using network-based graph community detection. Independent component analysis stabilised versions. Helper functions Hedge’s G effect size OpenTargets score summarisation.
